mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an abrupt contraction in consumer spending capacity, forcing equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of triggered instances, invalidating the recession-risk pricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases, breaking the demand-synchronization premise.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and triggers immediate positive equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 when CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above the 0.4% threshold force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing the probability of a hard landing and driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of high-inflation trigger months, contradicting the restrictive policy transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income, supporting durable goods consumption and corporate profit margins, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows, indicating no persistent earnings linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth validate synchronized demand across production and consumption sectors, confirming earnings resilience and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Co-occurring positive signals fail to predict positive returns in >55% of instances, breaking the synchronization assumption.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of industrial production contraction indicate a supply-side bottleneck or demand collapse, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive forward returns in >60% of triggered periods, invalidating the supply-contraction risk premium.
